arts.ttl: Arsenal 'handed goalkeeper transfer boost' as £4m release clause discount emerges
arts.sum: Arsenal-linked goalkeeper Joan Garcia reportedly sees his release clause reduced by €5m (£4.2m) after the Gunners were priced out of a move for him this summer.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta comments on William Saliba Arsenal red card and hints at appeal decision
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta offers his view on William Saliba's sending off in the Gunners' 2-0 Premier League loss to Bournemouth at the Vitality Stadium.

arts.ttl: Saliba CONTROVERSY in Arsenal loss, Man Utd PANICKED, Chelsea DISCOUNT
arts.sum: Saliba CONTROVERSY in Arsenal loss, Man Utd PANICKED, Chelsea DISCOUNT – FootballTransfers Recap Man Utd might have eased the pressure on Erik ten Hag as they overcame Brentford 2-1 at home, recovering from a 1-0 half-time deficit, but the long-term structural issues at Old Trafford persist.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta admits Arsenal miss star - and it's not Martin Odegaard or Bukayo Saka - football.london
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has been left ruing the unavailability of one of his Arsenal defenders, but was delighted that Ben White was able to return from injury against Bournemouth. Martin Odegaard ’s absence against the Cherries was expected but Bukayo Saka did not travel to the Vitality Stadium, and was left out of the matchday squad for the first time this season.

arts.ttl: Do Arsenal have a red card problem - and could it cost them the title?
arts.sum: Just eight games into the Premier League season and Arsenal have already had three players sent off - but just how costly could it be?

arts.ttl: Declan Rice blames 'silly mistakes' for Arsenal's shock loss at Bournemouth after William Saliba's costly red card - with midfielder admitting side 'kicked themselves in the foot' again with third dismissal of the season
arts.sum: ISAAN KHAN AT THE VITALITY STADIUM: Declan Rice admitted that 'silly mistakes' are hurting 'naïve' Arsenal as William Saliba's red card paved the way for Bournemouth to pull off a 2-0 victory.

arts.ttl: Basque curse for Arteta? Arsenal's rare defeat in numbers
arts.sum: It was only the second time that Bournemouth had beaten Arsenal in the Premier League, losing 11 of their past 14 meetings. But since Arteta's first game in charge of Arsenal, on Boxing Day 2019, the Gunners have been shown 18 red cards in the Premier League, at least five more than any other side.

arts.ttl: Arteta: We have to blame ourselves for Bournemouth defeat
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta said Arsenal have no plans to appeal William Saliba's red card against Bournemouth and insisted his side must blame themselves for the 2-0 loss on the south coast.

arts.ttl: Major Danny Welbeck update as former Arsenal star's horror injury left him needing oxygen
arts.sum: Speaking after his side's win at St James' Park, Brighton boss Fabian Hurzeler admitted he was unable to provide an update on Welbeck's condition. "Dat guy’s a bit bruised and battered, but the good news is our match-winner at St James’ Park, @DannyWelbeck, is on our @British_Airways flight home," read an update across the club's social media channels.

arts.ttl: Premier League release statement on David Raya penalty decision during Arsenal vs Bournemouth
arts.sum: It was confirmed that the spot-kick was awarded after the contact during the challenge by Raya on Evanilson was "sufficient" for the penalty to be given. Despite giving away a penalty, Raya was not handed a red card for the challenge on Evanlison due to the double jeopardy rule in the Premier League.
